topic: Agentic AI mitigates LLM hallucinations in academic literature reviews.
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in Large Language Models (LLMs) has been proposed as a potential solution to mitigate hallucinations in academic literature reviews (Clark, 2013). Hallucinations refer to the generation of false or inaccurate information by LLMs, which can be detrimental to the validity and reliability of academic research (Kasneci et al., 2023). Recent studies have explored the applications of LLMs in various fields, including healthcare education (Sallam, 2023; Alowais et al., 2023) and higher education (Rudolph et al., 2023; Grassini, 2023). The use of LLMs in these fields has also raised concerns regarding potential biases and inaccuracies (Feuerriegel et al., 2023).

The use of Agentic AI in LLMs can potentially address these concerns by enabling the models to engage in more situated and contextualized interactions (Clark, 2013). This can be achieved through the integration of retrieval-augmented generation techniques, which allow LLMs to retrieve and incorporate relevant information from external sources (Gao et al., 2023). Additionally, the development of autonomous agents based on LLMs can facilitate more efficient and effective information retrieval and processing (Wang et al., 2024). However, the lack of transparency and explainability in LLMs can make it difficult to identify and correct errors (Yan et al., 2023).

Despite the potential benefits of Agentic AI in mitigating hallucinations, there are still several challenges and limitations that need to be addressed. For example, the increasing reliance on LLMs in academic research raises concerns regarding the potential for bias and homogenization of ideas (Dwivedi et al., 2023). To address these challenges, it is essential to conduct further research on the applications and implications of Agentic AI in LLMs, as well as to develop more robust and transparent evaluation methods (Zawacki-Richter et al., 2019; OpenAI et al., 2023). Large language models have been found to encode clinical knowledge (Singhal et al., 2023), highlighting their potential in supporting academic research and education.

In conclusion, the integration of Agentic AI in LLMs has the potential to mitigate hallucinations in academic literature reviews. However, further research is needed to fully explore the benefits and limitations of this approach. By examining the existing literature and identifying areas for future research, it is possible to work towards developing more effective and reliable LLMs that can support academic research and education.
